title: 10-Minute Curd Semiya

subs.name: leftover cooked rice
subs.notes: effectively becomes curd rice — equally good

subs.name: thin coconut milk for a dairy-free version
subs.notes: serve immediately — coconut milk doesn't hold well

subs.name: omit if not available
subs.notes: the curry leaf tempering provides enough fragrance

steps.text: Bring 2 cups water to a boil with a pinch of salt. Add vermicelli and cook 3–4 minutes until just tender — it should have a very slight bite. Drain through a colander, rinse with cold water, and spread to cool.
Heat oil in a small pan on medium. Splutter mustard seeds, then add urad dal and fry until golden. Add curry leaves, green chilli, ginger, and asafoetida and fry 20 seconds until fragrant.
Pour the hot tempering immediately over the whisked curd and stir to combine — the curd will absorb the aromas.
Add the cooled vermicelli to the curd mixture and fold gently. Add salt to taste. The semiya will absorb the curd and thicken slightly — if it looks too thick, stir in 2 tablespoons of water or milk.
Finish with coriander leaves and serve at room temperature or lightly chilled. It holds well for a few hours, making it an excellent lunchbox option.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → thin coconut milk for a dairy-free version (serve immediately — coconut milk doesn't hold well); vermicelli → leftover cooked rice (effectively becomes curd rice — equally good); ginger → omit if not available (the curry leaf tempering provides enough fragrance)
